channel.title: NYT > U.S. News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Midsize Cities Held Steady as U.S. Population Growth Declined
item.description: New census estimates show that the nation’s largest cities saw the sharpest slowdowns in population growth over the past year amid declines in immigration and plunging birthrates.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra
item.media:credit: Stacy Sodolak for The New York Times
item.media:description: Austin, Texas, grew to have one million residents, but overall, the growth of midsize cities outpaced that of the larger ones near them.

item.title: South Carolina Governor Plans Special Session to Redraw House Maps
item.description: Gov. Henry McMaster, a Republican, appears prepared to thrust the state into the nation’s redistricting wars.
item.dc:creator: Eduardo Medina
item.media:description: Gov. Henry McMaster’s decision to call back the state legislature came a day after the State Senate voted to block a resolution to do so.

item.title: U.S. Cruise Passenger Moved Out of Isolation
item.description: The passenger, who had tested positive once in the Canary Islands, was being monitored in a special biocontainment facility in Omaha, until a new test came back negative.
item.dc:creator: Sonia A. Rao and Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:description: American passengers from the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship were being monitored at the Nebraska Medical Center in Omaha.

item.title: 11 Rescued From Raft After Plane Crash Off the Coast of Florida
item.description: Those on board, who were all Bahamian adults, spent about five hours stranded on a raft before rescuers hoisted them to safety. All were in stable condition, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ziegler
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Eleven people survived a plane crash on Tuesday off the coast of Melbourne, Fla., and clung to a raft for several hours before rescuers arrived, the authorities said.

item.title: Justice Dept. Sues D.C. Bar Over Efforts to Discipline Government Lawyers
item.description: The move escalates the administration’s feud with legal disciplinary bodies over the ethical conduct of government lawyers.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
item.media:description: Todd Blanche, the acting attorney general, accused the D.C. Bar, which handles complaints of lawyer misconduct, of acting as a “blatantly partisan arm of leftist causes.”

item.title: Denise Powell Wins Democratic Primary in Key Nebraska House Race
item.description: Denise Powell prevailed in a close primary race for an Omaha-based seat, currently held by a retiring Republican, that could help determine control of the House.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning
item.media:description: Denise Powell narrowly won a Democratic House primary that featured an unusual argument: that electing her chief party rival, State Senator John Cavanaugh, could make it easier for Republicans to win the White House in 2028.

item.title: House Approves Year-Round Sale of Higher-Ethanol Gas
item.description: In a rare bipartisan vote, the House passed a bill allowing for the sale of an ethanol blend known as E15, over the objections of Republican hard-liners and some oil refiners.
item.dc:creator: Linda Qiu
item.media:credit: Jordan Gale for The New York Times
item.media:description: An ethanol processing plant in Wellsburg, Iowa, in 2023.

item.title: Second Venezuelan Doctor Is Released From Immigration Custody
item.description: At least five foreign-born doctors have been detained, according to a medical organization, raising fears that underserved rural areas reliant on them could be further strained.
item.dc:creator: Miriam Jordan

item.title: The China Gambit: From Nixon to Trump
item.description: President Trump’s visit to Beijing comes as relations with China have become more combative.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong
item.media:description: President Richard M. Nixon visiting Beijing in 1972.

item.title: New Mexico Diocese Tries to Block Government From Seizing Land for Border Wall
item.description: The diocese’s lawyers argued that the land, which is below a 29-foot-tall statue of Jesus Christ on a mountain peak, is a holy site protected under religious freedom.
item.dc:creator: Max Bearak
item.media:description: A statue of Christ atop Mount Cristo Rey with a view of Mexico on the left and Sunland Park, N.M., on the right.

item.title: Duffy’s ‘Great American Road Trip’ Prompts Ethical Concerns
item.description: A YouTube series starring Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy and his family is part of the nation’s 250th anniversary celebrations, but it doubled as a family excursion for them.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian
item.category: Citizens for Responsibility and Ethics in Washington
item.media:credit: Victor J. Blue for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mr. Duffy has insisted that the department’s career ethics and budget officials reviewed and approved his participation in the YouTube series.

item.title: How Jerome Powell Managed a Chaotic Era
item.description: Our reporter Colby Smith, who covers the Federal Reserve and the economy, looks back at Jerome Powell’s tenure as chair of the Fed.
item.dc:creator: Colby Smith, Nour Idriss, Stephanie Swart and Rafaela Balster

item.title: Trump Administration Will Withhold $1.3 Billion in Medicaid Payments to California
item.description: Vice President JD Vance said the state had not done enough to fight fraud in the public health insurance program.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kliff
item.category: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance said that California had failed to combat fraud in Medicaid.

item.title: Miami Residents Sue Over Trump’s Plan to Make His Library a Hotel
item.description: The suit argues that the development on state-donated land would violate the Constitution’s domestic emoluments clause, which prohibits the president from accepting money or gifts from states.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei
item.media:credit: Alfonso Duran for The New York Times
item.media:description: Land in downtown Miami donated by the State of Florida to President Trump is now at the center of a lawsuit filed in federal court on Wednesday.

item.title: Homeland Security Dept. Escalates Its Dispute With Federal Judge
item.description: In a column, the top lawyer for D.H.S. claimed that the judge, Melissa DuBose, was engaging in an “intimidation campaign” by referring a government lawyer for a misconduct investigation.
item.dc:creator: Mattathias Schwartz and Hamed Aleaziz
item.media:description: Judge Melissa R. DuBose referred an administration lawyer for possible discipline last week after he acknowledged that he had failed to inform her that a migrant she was preparing to release from detention was facing homicide charges in the Dominican Republic.

item.title: Trump’s Remark on Americans’ Finances and Iran War Points to GOP Midterm Challenges
item.description: Asked if he was motivated by Americans’ financial woes to make a deal to end the war with Iran, he responded, “Not even a little bit.”
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times

item.title: Kouri Richins Sentenced to Life in Prison for Poisoning Her Husband
item.description: Kouri Richins, 36, who wrote a children’s book about grief after murdering Eric Richins, will serve without the option of parole.
item.dc:creator: Remy Tumin
item.media:description: Kouri Richins, right, at a sentencing hearing in Park City, Utah, on Wednesday.

item.title: Georgia Governor Brian Kemp Calls Special Session to Redistrict for 2028 Elections
item.description: Gov. Brian Kemp, a Republican, also asked lawmakers to delay changes to the state’s election system that could cause disarray in the midterms.
item.dc:creator: Rick Rojas
item.media:description: Gov. Brian Kemp of Georgia on Wednesday called lawmakers back to the State Capitol next month to redraw the state’s legislative districts for the 2028 election cycle.

item.title: Uyghurs Hope Trump Returns to First-Term Toughness at Xi Meeting
item.description: In 2021, the president declared China’s crackdown on Uyghurs a genocide. Now, it rarely surfaces.
item.dc:creator: Amy Qin
item.media:credit: Elizabeth Frantz for The New York Times
item.media:description: A protest in support of East Turkestan, the name some Uyghurs use for the Chinese region of Xinjiang, outside the White House last year.

item.title: Gen. Caine’s Silence on Iran War Leaves Questions About Military Strategy
item.description: The president’s top military adviser is walking a tightrope as he leads the military through a divisive and unpopular war.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: In public, Gen. Dan Caine has avoided discussing with lawmakers the broader U.S. military strategy.

item.title: Hantavirus Attacks Patients’ Bodies. This Doctor Tends to Their Minds.
item.description: David Cates, a psychologist working with Americans exposed to the disease on the MV Hondius, said psychosocial supports are just as important as physical care.
item.dc:creator: Sonia A. Rao
item.media:description: Nebraska Medicine’s Davis Global Center, where American passengers from the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship are quarantining.

item.title: Senate Confirms Kevin Warsh as Fed Chair
item.description: President Trump’s pick to replace Jerome H. Powell takes the helm at a highly uncertain moment for the economy and with heightened concern about the Federal Reserve’s ability to operate independently.
item.dc:creator: Colby Smith
item.media:description: Kevin M. Warsh has vowed to embark on “regime change” at the Federal Reserve.

item.title: Stephen Cloobeck, Former California Governor Candidate, Arrested in Los Angeles County
item.description: Stephen Cloobeck, a wealthy developer who dropped out of the governor’s race in November, was arrested on suspicion of witness tampering in a felony case involving his fiancée, a former Penthouse model.
item.dc:creator: Tim Arango
item.media:credit: Wade Vandervort/Las Vegas Sun, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Stephen Cloobeck was arrested on Tuesday in Los Angeles and briefly jailed on suspicion of witness tampering.

item.title: Eric Trump, Who Runs the Family Business, Hops Along on the Plane to China
item.description: The president’s middle son is said to be joining his father in a “personal capacity” rather than to make business deals.
item.dc:creator: Ben Protess
item.media:description: President Trump and his delegation, including his son Eric Trump, at the Beijing Capital Airport on Wednesday.

item.title: Chinese Firms Plot Secret Arms Sales to Iran, U.S. Officials Say
item.description: The effort involves plans to send weapons through other countries in an effort to hide the origins of the shipments.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes, Mark Mazzetti and Dustin Volz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump arrived in Beijing on Wednesday for a high-stakes diplomatic visit with President Xi Jinping of China.

item.title: Trump Administration Pushes I.R.S. to Identify Undocumented Immigrants
item.description: The deliberations are a sign that the Trump administration may once again try to harness the power of the I.R.S. to advance its immigration agenda.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Duehren and Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.media:credit: Mark Abramson for The New York Times
item.media:description: A tax preparation clinic in Los Angeles. The Internal Revenue Service has in effect looked the other way on immigration status, focusing instead on encouraging people to pay the taxes that they may owe.

item.title: ICE Detains Ex-Kansas Mayor Joe Ceballos Who Voted Illegally
item.description: Joe Ceballos said he did not know he was ineligible to vote and run for office as a green-card holder. The Trump administration has drawn attention to the case.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith
item.media:credit: Clayton Steward for The New York Times
item.media:description: Former Mayor Joe Ceballos of Coldwater, Kan., was born in Mexico and came to the United States when he was 4 years old.

item.title: Billionaire Rick Jackson Is Upending the Georgia Governor’s Race
item.description: Rick Jackson was largely unknown to voters just a few months ago. Heading into Tuesday’s Republican primary, he’s keeping some of the state’s best-known politicians on their toes.
item.dc:creator: Rick Rojas, Katie Benner and Michelle Baruchman
item.category: Georgia Criminal Case Against Trump (2020 Election)
item.media:credit: Dustin Chambers for The New York Times
item.media:description: Rick Jackson, a billionaire health care executive with no government experience, was largely unknown to Georgia voters. Now, he has a decent chance of being elected governor in November.

item.title: For Trump, the Iran War Looms Over China Summit
item.description: The balance of power between the United States and China had shifted in Beijing’s favor even before the war in Iran began in February.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Donald J. Trump leaving the White House on Tuesday. He arrived in Beijing today.

item.title: Rich Danker, RFK Jr.’s Top Spokesman, Resigns in Protest Over New Vaping Policy
item.description: In a letter to President Trump, the spokesman, Rich Danker, said allowing the sale of flavored e-cigarettes would enhance their appeal to children.
item.dc:creator: Sheryl Gay Stolberg
item.media:credit: Michael A. McCoy for The New York Times
item.media:description: The resignation comes at a fraught time for the Department of Health and Human Services, which now has three major vacancies.

item.title: Iran War Powers Vote Fails Again in Senate, but GOP Opposition Grows
item.description: Senator Lisa Murkowski of Alaska became the latest Republican to switch her vote to halt the conflict and require President Trump to win congressional approval to continue it.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Senator Lisa Murkowski, Republican of Alaska, during a committee hearing. She previously opposed similar resolutions.

item.title: Trump and Xi: Beneath the Pomp and Niceties, a Geopolitical Rivalry
item.description: The relationship between the two leaders is marked just as much by mistrust and confrontation as it is by niceties.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.media:description: President Trump and President Xi Jinping of China after a meeting in Busan, South Korea, last year. They are set to meet again this week.

item.title: House Backers Reach Threshold to Force Vote on $1.3 Billion in Ukraine Aid
item.description: Representative Kevin Kiley, independent of California, became the 218th signature on a petition to force action on an aid bill to Ukraine. The vote could come as soon as the end of May.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.media:description: Representative Kevin Kiley signed a discharge petition calling for a vote, starting a clock that will compel House leaders to bring the measure to the floor as soon as the end of the month.

item.title: Musk, Rubio, Hegseth: Who Is Traveling With Trump to China?
item.description: President Trump arrived in Beijing, ahead of his meeting with President Xi Jinping of China. Among those accompanying Mr. Trump were top administration officials and business leaders.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kwai

item.title: N.Y.U. Students Object to Graduation Speaker Jonathan Haidt, Who Calls Their Generation ‘Coddled’
item.description: Jonathan Haidt, a professor, says that colleges shield students from challenging ideas. But student leaders said he does not represent their values.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy W. Peters and Matthew Haag
item.category: The Coddling of the American Mind: How Good Intentions and Bad Ideas Are Setting Up a Generation for Failure (Book)
item.media:credit: Lila Barth for The New York Times
item.media:description: Jonathan Haidt at New York University in 2024.

item.title: Trump Arrives in Beijing to Begin High-Stakes Summit With Xi
item.description: President Trump was greeted by China’s vice president, Han Zheng, and other officials, as well as a military honor guard.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump arrived in Beijing on Wednesday.

item.title: With a Friend in Trump, the Tobacco Industry Secures a Lucrative Win
item.description: In a dispute over vapes, the president sided with tobacco companies that filled his groups’ coffers over his own F.D.A. commissioner, who resigned in protest.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett and Kenneth P. Vogel
item.media:description: The F.D.A. issued new guidance that could pave the way for major tobacco companies to begin selling flavored vapes less than a week after industry executives met with President Trump.

item.title: Alex Murdaugh Murder Convictions Overturned by South Carolina’s Top Court
item.description: The decision, in which judges cited jury interference by a court clerk, upends one of America’s highest-profile homicide cases.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Bogel-Burroughs
item.media:description: Alex Murdaugh, convicted of killing his wife, Maggie, and younger son, Paul, in June 2021, sits during a hearing on a motion for a retrial in 2024.

item.title: Where Did All the AK-47s Go?
item.description: A family of guns that was once ubiquitous in the U.S. firearms marketplace has started to vanish for a variety of reasons.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Gibbons-Neff
item.media:credit: Adriana Zehbrauskas for The New York Times
item.media:description: Jim Fuller, who builds AK-47-style rifles, in his office in Scottsdale, Ariz.

item.title: Corpus Christi Faces Water Crisis as Drought and Industrial Growth Strain Supply
item.description: Rising demand, municipal dysfunction and drought have pushed Corpus Christi to the edge of a water emergency, offering a cautionary tale for the rest of the country.
item.dc:creator: Lauren McGaughy and Meridith Kohut
item.media:description: Corpus Christi’s industrial port has been expanding, but new water sources have not been keeping pace, especially amid a drought that has stretched on far longer than local officials expected.
